question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
Move R3's R3↔R2 link (GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0) from OSPF Area 0 to
Area 1; R2 keeps the link in Area 0. The two routers can no longer
form an OSPF adjacency over the link — neighbor state drops from FULL
and an ADJCHG syslog fires on both sides referencing 192.0.0.9.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R3, interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (the R3<->R2 link) was moved from OSPF area 0 into area 1, while R2 keeps that link in area 0.
The two ends now disagree on the area ID for the link; OSPF requires both neighbors on a link to share the same area ID, so each end rejects the other's Hellos, the R2<->R3 adjacency over GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 cannot form, and no routes are exchanged across that link.
Fix: return R3's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 to OSPF area 0.
expected_symptoms_summary:
OSPF adjacency state change (%ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG):
- R2: neighbor 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) EXSTART → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) 2WAY → DOWN reason "interface down or detached".
- Bilateral: both ends (R2, R3) report the transition.

OSPF packet receive error (%ROUTING-OSPF-4-ERRRCV):
- R3: mismatch area ID from peer 192.0.0.9 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0.
- R3: mismatch area ID from peer 192.0.0.9 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0.
- R3: mismatch area ID from peer 192.0.0.9 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0.

BGP session state change (%ROUTING-BGP-5-ADJCHANGE):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4.
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.7.
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.3.

%ROUTING-BGP-5-NSR_STATE_CHANGE reported by R3, R4, R5 (3 instances):
- R3: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
- R4: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready
- R5: Changed state to Not NSR-Ready

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
no interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
area 1
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 message-digest-key 1 md5 cisco123

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 1
no interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0
 message-digest-key 1 md5 cisco123

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 13:39:01.768 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.2 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from 2WAY to DOWN, Neighbor Down: interface down or detached, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000
